features.evidence: 2 gen 2 with a display port signal and power delivery and the power connector of course wireless transfers are a bit on the slower side and the webcam is well not the greatest but given that even higher notebooks come with less than stellar cameras i think we can give hp a pass here in the maintenance department you have easy access to a pair of sodum and nvme slots for any future upgrades you may have planned once again both the keyboard and the touchpad are okay while the general ta ility is solid given the figure chassis we would have preferred more travel and maybe a more pronounced pressure press point for some added feedback in games overall though you get a good typing experience with a well spaced out layout the large track pad does its job as well even though the clicks feel a little bit hollow if you are bothered about that thing on the display side hp continues its apparently very deliberate middle of the road approach the 16x9 100 srgb panel comes well calibrated out of the box with good response times but it doesn t get terribly bright and also lacks a little in the contrast department other contenders in the same price pet do better in this regard lenovo s legion 5 notebooks for example but for a mid range gamer it is still a very capable display that does not have any apparent flaws that would distract you from your favorite games in the performance department hp also opted for reduced power levels for both the ryzen zpu and the nvidia gpu instead of going full tilt once more to give you a more balanced experience when it comes to fps and fan noise to that end at least when it comes to cpu performance the omen 17 is one of the slowest machines within this class of devices and even notebooks the xmg core 15 which is a lot smaller and comes with the same cpu can perform a lot better here but pure cpu grunts is not everything and in our system performance benchmark the large 17 inr already performs a lot better and even subjectively you will not run into any very obvious performance bottlenecks with the omen 17 the 1 tb ssd is a little slow by today s standard but again if you would really notice that especially when using this primarily as a gaming machine is a very different story especially since transfer rates remain stable even during our stress tests even on the gpu side the rtx 4070 runs about 20 wats below it s allowed maximum tgp of 140 wats but the omen 17 shows very impressively that this does not mean much when it comes to real world performance it can easily outperform everything in our synthetic tests our 1080p rating puts things back into perspective and since these are more cpu bound games the slower rizon is definitely a disadvantage for the large gaming notebook here but in something more gpu bound the omen can once again gain some crown against a similarly equipped competition and in general you will not find a lot of even more demanding games that drop below the magical 60 fps mark in the display s native resolution and if they do well you still got dlss and frame ch to help you out here i teased it before the reduced power levels and overall tuning of the omen 17 mean some tremendous benefits in the fan noise department so please enjoy our noise samples and for any additional information when it comes to temperatures and our stress test results for example please check out our written review on the website battery life is very impressive for such a large notebook and once again shows the benefits of the more restrained tuning at higher brightness levels the large display demands quite a bit more power though but again for a big 17 in gamer those numbers are definitely quite okay but that should be it for today so what do we think about the all new omen 17 well i think hp is set out to put together a gaming notebook that delivers a very good and sometimes even a great gaming experience without sounding a jet engine and they absolutely nailed it getting there did require some compromises for sure but they are well balanced and overall the omen 17 is a strong contender in this mid range class of larger gaming notebooks but as always let me know what you think sound of in the comments below thanks a ton for watching my name is alex please do not forget to leave your and sub on your way out and i cannot wait to see you all in the next one take care

hp has been offering gaming laptops under its omen brand for a while now and for the past few years you have been able to get them with the fastest core components available in our testing though they may have been solid and provided a good gaming experience however it seems they could not always keep up with the rest of the high end competition so with the all new omen 17 they tried something a little different and for all of you who think modern laptops are too thin and too small to be adequately cooled and keep a low noise profile well hp has hurt you as well and went exactly the other way with this one right here in front of me they have put together a very capable gaming notebook that is neither the fastest nor the finest nor comes with any other fancy features but it runs all the latest games very well and manages to stay quiet and well cooled our sample represents the fastest configuration of the redesigned omen it comes with amd s ryzen 7 8845 hs and a nidia s rtx 4070 running at a max tgp of 120 wats add in a generous amount of ram in this price range a 1 tb ssd and a pretty snappy qhd display and you get a well equipped gamer for way below 2,000 bucks the new omen follows the same design principles as as predecessors and we get a relatively subtle chassis with very minimal branding it s plastic but i don t think that this is necessarily a problem here since the base is still very rigid with only minimal flex and no great breaking or anything the lid is doing quite well to keep the large panel in place but there is still a little bit of wobble when you adjust it to your desired viewing position as i mentioned the 17in is quite large and heavy as well so you should definitely check if it would fit your backpack again between the pleora of gaming laptops that sacrifice cooling potential and noise for a thinner chassis i think hp might be onto something here the port situation is a bit of a mixed back even though we still have to keep the price point in mind here so while the overall selection is great you will not always get the fastest variant of each port on the left you get your audio port and a 1 gbit network check in the same outof theway placement the single usba on the right it is of the slower 5 gb variant but it s perfectly located for your mouse dongles or the behind back you get another pair of usb this time in the faster 3

review_summary: 2 gen 2 with a display port signal and power delivery and the power connector of course wireless transfers are a bit on the slower side and the webcam is well not the greatest but given that even higher notebooks come with less than stellar cameras i think we can give hp a pass here in the maintenance department you have easy access to a pair of sodum and nvme slots for any future upgrades you may have planned once again both the keyboard and the touchpad are okay while the general ta ility is solid given the figure chassis we would have preferred more travel and maybe a more pronounced pressure press point for some added feedback in games overall though you get a good typing experience with a well spaced out layout the large track pad does its job as well even though the clicks feel a little bit hollow if you are bothered about that thing on the display side hp continues its apparently very deliberate middle of the road approach the 16x9 100 srgb panel comes well calibrated out of the box with good response times but it doesn t get terribly bright and also lacks a little in the contrast department other contenders in the same price pet do better in this regard lenovo s legion 5 notebooks for example but for a mid range gamer it is still a very capable display that does not have any apparent flaws that would distract you from your favorite games in the performance department hp also opted for reduced power levels for both the ryzen zpu and the nvidia gpu instead of going full tilt once more to give you a more balanced experience when it comes to fps and fan noise to that end at least when it comes to cpu performance the omen 17 is one of the slowest machines within this class of devices and even notebooks the xmg core 15 which is a lot smaller and comes with the same cpu can perform a lot better here but pure cpu grunts is not everything and in our system performance benchmark the large 17 inr already performs a lot better and even subjectively you will not run into any very obvious performance bottlenecks with the omen 17 the 1 tb ssd is a little slow by today s standard but again if you would really notice that especially when using this primarily as a gaming machine is a very different story especially since transfer rates remain stable even during our stress tests even on the gpu side the rtx 4070 runs about 20 wats below it s allowed maximum tgp of 140 wats but the omen 17 shows very impressively that this does not mean much when it comes to real world performance it can easily outperform everything in our synthetic tests our 1080p rating puts things back into perspective and since these are more cpu bound games the slower rizon is definitely a disadvantage for the large gaming notebook here but in something more gpu bound the omen can once again gain some crown against a similarly equipped competition and in general you will not find a lot of even more demanding games that drop below the magical 60 fps mark in the display s native resolution and if they do well you still got dlss and frame ch to help you out here i teased it before the reduced power levels and overall tuning of the omen 17 mean some tremendous benefits in the fan noise department so please enjoy our noise samples and for any additional information when it comes to temperatures and our stress test results for example please check out our written review on the website battery life is very impressive for such a large notebook and once again shows the benefits of the more restrained tuning at higher brightness levels the large display demands quite a bit more power though but again for a big 17 in gamer those numbers are definitely quite okay but that should be it for today so what do we think about the all new omen 17 well i think hp is set out to put together a gaming notebook that delivers a very good and sometimes even a great gaming experience without sounding a jet engine and they absolutely nailed it getting there did require some compromises for sure but they are well balanced and overall the omen 17 is a strong contender in this mid range class of larger gaming notebooks but as always let me know what you base is still very rigid with only minimal flex and no great breaking or anything the lid is doing quite well to keep the large panel in place but there is still a little bit of wobble when you adjust it to your desired viewing position as i mentioned the 17in is quite large and heavy as well so you should definitely check if it would fit your backpack again between the pleora of gaming laptops that sacrifice cooling potential and noise for a thinner chassis i think hp might be onto something here the port situation is a bit of a mixed back even though we still have to keep the price point in mind here so while the overall selection is great you will not always get the fastest variant of each port on the left you get your audio port and a 1 gbit network check in the same outof theway placement the single usba on the right it is of the slower 5 gb variant but it s perfectly located for your mouse dongles or the behind back you get another pair of usb this time in the faster 3
